eval_name: Tilt detection identifies type, cites evidence, prescribes intervention
prompt: I just played 5 games in a row after losing my first game. Each game I played faster and more aggressively. Here are my times per move: [20s, 15s, 8s, 4s, 2s average]

assertions.rubric: Does it classify the type of tilt (e.g. frustration tilt, speed tilt, aggression tilt, revenge tilt)?
Does it reference the pattern of decreasing move times or increasing aggression as evidence for the diagnosis?

eval_name: Session check recommends play/study/rest with clear reasoning
prompt: I want to start a chess session. I slept 7 hours, ate well, feel slightly stressed from work. Should I play, study, or rest?

assertions.rubric: Does it explicitly recommend one of: play, study, or rest as the primary activity?
Does it explain why it chose that recommendation based on the provided conditions (sleep, nutrition, stress level)?
Does it specifically acknowledge the work stress as a factor in the recommendation?

eval_name: Time analysis detects time trouble and classifies move speeds
prompt: Analyse my time usage in this game. I had 10 minutes each. Moves 1-15: 20s avg, moves 16-25: 45s avg, moves 26-35: 8s avg, moves 36-40: 2s avg.

assertions.rubric: Does it identify that the player entered time trouble in the later phase of the game?
Does it distinguish between different speed phases or segments of the game (e.g. normal, slow, rushed, blitz-like)?
